channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: A device saved Rory's life. Now Canberrans can access one in every suburb
item.description: Rory was spending his night like any other 16-year-old, playing sports with friends, when the unthinkable happened. He went into cardiac arrest.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Roberts

media:group.media:description: Basketball player Rory Pittman is now a volunteer for St Johns Ambulance after suffering a sudden cardiac arrest at 16.

item.title: Kyle and Jackie O debacle costs ARN Media $26m in lost revenue
item.description: ARN Media reveals "brand safety" concerns over the Kyle and Jackie O Show have cost the company tens of millions in advertising revenue.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Kylie Sandilands and Jackie Henderson were set to earn a combined $200 million over 10 years.

item.title: Gas bills 'no longer hostage' to international market under new laws to drop price
item.description: A new east coast gas reserve will "drive down" prices, according to the federal government and ensure Australia is no longer "hostage" to international markets.
item.dc:creator: Jane Norman

media:group.media:description: The long-promised reservation policy is designed to avert looming east coast gas shortages.

item.title: Pilot killed in remote chopper crash not qualified for night flight, inquiry finds
item.description: An ATSB investigation into a fatal helicopter crash found the pilot was flying after sunset without the correct qualifications or equipment, prompting new safety procedures at the remote cattle station where he was working.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson

media:group.media:description: A Robinson R22 helicopter like the one that crashed. The image has been digitally altered by the ATSB.

item.title: Carnarvon shire council will receive commissioner after mass resignations
item.description: WA's local government minister will appoint a commissioner to Carnarvon Shire following mass council resignations.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Stanley

media:group.media:description: A commissioner will be appointed to the Shire of Carnarvon, following six out of eight councillors resigning.

item.title: This WA town is crawling with mice and residents want help
item.description: A severe mouse plague in the farming town of Morawa overwhelms homes, businesses and farms, prompting urgent calls for government assistance amid growing health concerns and rising economic losses.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Prendergast and Chris Lewis

media:group.media:description: Mice are moving from grain farms surrounding Morawa into the town.

item.title: 'Fear for their life': New SA powers to ban abusive customers from stores
item.description: Aggressive and violent customers could be barred from supermarket chains and shopping centres under sweeping South Australian laws that have come into effect this week.
item.dc:creator: Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Retail worker Janine Hall has welcomed new laws banning violent shoppers.

item.title: British PM Keir Starmer braces for ballot box backlash
item.description: They might be local elections, but the results of thousands of votes being held on Thursday around Britain are likely to have major ramifications for the national government.
item.dc:creator: Mazoe Ford

media:group.media:description: Sir Keir Starmer and his Labour government in Britain are struggling in the polls.

item.title: Melbourne man was high on synthetic drug when he murdered friend
item.description: A man who was high on an illegal, synthetic drug when he murdered his friend has been jailed, with a judge describing the case as "most unusual".
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Mark Ludbrook will be eligible for parole in 2038.

item.title: Victorian Coalition vows to pause transmission projects if it wins state election
item.description: The Victorian opposition has revealed its renewable energy plan ahead of the state election, vowing to pause major transmission projects and establish urban solar parks.
item.dc:creator: Angus Verley and Timu King

media:group.media:description: Sights like this are common on farms in the path of VNI West and WRL.

item.title: Trio sentenced after forcing space engineers to dig dog's grave in an 'ambush'
item.description: Three bush camp residents have been spared further jail time after forcing five space base engineers to bury a dog believed to have been killed by a hire car in remote Australia.
item.dc:creator: Conor Byrne

media:group.media:description: Luke Doyel Drummond has been sentenced to 18 months jail, released immediately on parole.

item.title: Police investigating after woman's 'unexplained' death at Henley Beach
item.description: Police were called to the Esplanade at Henley Beach about 6:20am after the body of a woman was found at the beachside suburb.

media:group.media:description: SA Police are investigating after a woman's body was found at Henley Beach.

item.title: Experts warn increasing self-reliance comes at a cost
item.description: With the war in the Middle East focusing attention on fuel reserves and Australia's self-reliance, experts warn there is no quick, or cheap, fix to reducing dependence on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Emma Wynne

media:group.media:description: Andrew Hastie says Australia needs to invest in becoming more self-reliant.

item.title: Leaders call for child protection reviews ahead of Kumanjayi Little Baby vigils
item.description: As memorials for Kumanjayi Little Baby are held across the country, leaders call for a review of the child protection system to ensure the safety of young people is prioritised.
item.dc:creator: Grace Atta

media:group.media:description: A shrine for five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby remains outside Old Timers camp in Alice Springs.

item.title: Homeless people's belongings can be seized under new Vic council laws
item.description: The Port Phillip council will enable authorities to impound the belongings of a person experiencing homelessness if they display anti-social behaviour.
item.dc:creator: Mikaela Ortolan

media:group.media:description: Rough sleepers are commonplace near social service providers in St Kilda.

item.title: Modi's state election win shows how things have changed in India
item.description: India's Narendra Modi has won a crucial state, shoring up his party, decimating the opposition, and almost establishing the ruling Bhartiya Janata Party as a pan-Indian presence.
item.dc:creator: Neelima Choahan

media:group.media:description: Narendra Modi says the latest election shows India's democracy is vibrant.

item.title: New gas field in waters near Twelve Apostles given green light
item.description: The gas field off the south-west coast of Victoria could provide up to 4 per cent of the east coast's energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: Jean Bell

media:group.media:description: The gas field will be in waters near the Twelve Apostles, which attracts more than 2.8 million visitors per year.

item.title: Bushfires now burn at a 'whole different level'. It means rethinking volunteers
item.description: Bushfires are occurring at a new level of ferocity across Australia. It could leave our volunteers overpowered.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Volunteer fire brigades across the state are trying to recruit members ahead of a hot dry summer.

item.title: After 17 years, Jason Taumalolo is still forging new legends
item.description: As he prepares to become the most capped player in North Queensland history, Cowboys veteran Jason Taumalolo has come back from the brink to forge one last legend in his glittering career.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Jason Taumalolo will claim his own piece of North Queensland Cowboys history on Friday.

item.title: Former Test cricketer was drinking wine before breath test, lawyer says
item.description: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner will accept responsibility for a "reckless", "foolish" and "wrong" decision that resulted in a mid-range drink driving charge, his lawyer says.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: David Warner is yet to enter a plea.

item.title: Judge releases note purportedly penned in jail by Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: A note Jeffrey Epstein's cellmate claims he found after the convicted sex offender's first suspected attempt to take his own life in a New York jail has been made public.

media:group.media:description: A note supposedly written by Jeffrey Epstein before his first attempt to take his own life in jail has been made public.

item.title: Jewish hospital patient 'paralysed with fear' after threatening video
item.description: A Jewish NSW Health worker and patient who says she experienced antisemitism in the workplace tells the royal commission she considered cancelling her knee surgery after becoming "paralysed" with fear in the wake of two nurses making threats against Israeli patients.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: AAV told the royal commission of incidents she experienced as a NSW Health staff member and patient.

item.title: Federation Square to host World Cup events after premier's intervention
item.description: Screenings of the FIFA World Cup will go ahead at Federation Square later this year after the Victorian premier overturned an unpopular decision to not host fans at the precinct.

media:group.media:description: Matildas fans at Federation Square in 2023 ahead of the Women's World Cup.

item.title: Major data breach sees student details compromised
item.description: Education institutions around the country are scrambling to respond to a global data breach that's affected Australian universities, TAFE and state schools in Queensland.

media:group.media:description: Canvas is a learning management software used by educational facilities across the world.

item.title: Passengers say 'ghost bookings' now common on crucial V/Line service
item.description: Public transport passengers in Victoria's north-west say a rise in "ghost bookings" has led to half-empty bus services during the state's free transport period.
item.dc:creator: Grace Marshall

media:group.media:description: The V/Line coach from Mildura is the only affordable option for many to travel to Melbourne.
